hard_negative_inventory.class: thenable assimilation and custom Promise-like receivers
executor callback timing and thrown executor errors
Go direct call versus goroutine/defer scheduling and callback effects
Go channel receive value/status, channel send, and select/default readiness boundaries
Python imported asyncio bindings shadowed by parameters, assignments, nested imports, or project-local asyncio modules
Rust brace/direct-imported runtime bindings shadowed by parameters, lets, local macros, block scopes, other modules, or project-local runtime roots
Swift structured-concurrency runtime names shadowed by local Task bindings, Task extensions, same-file task-group functions, or project-visible task-group functions
Java CompletableFuture static calls without exact stdlib type identity, or with local/conflicting type names
Java CompletionStage-style receiver continuations without import-backed java.util.concurrent type-domain evidence
Java Future local receivers with wildcard-only imports, reassignment, local shadows, or conflicting imports, plus wildcard-only Executor local receivers
Java Future field receivers that are implicit, non-this, member-shadowed, duplicate, or conflicting, plus conflicting Executor field receivers

policy.go_channel_operation_pricing: Directional channel type arrows such as <-chan and chan<- are excluded from send/receive operation counts.
policy.note: Counts price boundary surfaces; they do not prove exact semantics.

recommended_order.next_action: reporting-only split and hard-negative expansion before exact admission
recommended_order.why: Promise aggregate semantics are already corpus-priced and have adjacent first/all-settled hard negatives.

surfaces.note: await is scheduling and thenable assimilation, not sync value equivalence
Swift throws/try is an explicit error channel
defer has scope-exit ordering and panic interaction semantics
async functions have scheduling and rejection boundaries even without explicit await
Swift await has task scheduling and actor/lifetime boundaries
Rust .await polls a Future and must keep wake/scheduling effects explicit
Go channel receives have blocking, synchronization, and close/zero-value channel semantics
raise/rescue changes error channels and non-local control
Swift async surfaces create task/future-like protocol boundaries
Go select cases require readiness, case-selection, and send/receive side-effect proof
Executor/Future APIs introduce scheduler and lifecycle boundaries
async fn creates a suspended async function boundary
yield has suspension and iterator lifecycle semantics
Java streams need lazy/eager lifecycle and terminal materialization proof
go statements spawn concurrent execution
select has readiness, default, and scheduling semantics
Python await has coroutine scheduling and exception channel semantics
async def creates coroutine protocol boundaries
Go channel sends have blocking and synchronization semantics
async blocks create suspended async boundaries
Ruby yield invokes a block with demand/effect obligations
new Promise needs executor timing, resolve/reject callback, and throw-to-rejection contracts
Go select defaults change blocking and liveness behavior
async for/with needs awaitable lifecycle and cleanup proof
Promise.all needs ordered all-fulfilled value and first rejection semantics
async spawn APIs introduce scheduler, cancellation, and join-handle boundaries
CompletableFuture needs success/error channel and scheduling proof
AbortSignal/AbortController can change scheduling and rejection outcomes
Task APIs introduce scheduler, cancellation, and handle lifecycle boundaries
Exact-import-backed Java Future receivers expose blocking settled-value and exception channels
Exact-import-backed Java Future receivers expose cancellation and task-handle liveness boundaries
Exact-import-backed Java Executor receivers schedule callback execution without returning a task handle
Swift Task.sleep creates a timer-backed scheduling boundary and cancellation/liveness boundary
Go comma-ok receives expose the channel close status as an additional protocol result
Swift task groups need all-completion, result-channel, cancellation/liveness, and throwing error-channel proof
Exact-import-backed Java ExecutorService receivers schedule callbacks and return Future handles
Exact-import-backed Java Future receivers expose task-handle lifecycle status
asyncio sleep creates a timer-backed scheduling boundary
Thread/Fiber APIs create scheduler and lifecycle boundaries
setInterval and timers interval streams have repeated emission, cancellation, and liveness semantics
pthread_create introduces thread scheduling and lifetime boundaries
qualified runtime join style macros need all-completion result-channel proof
Swift throwing continuation bridges add exception-channel behavior to callback-settled future-like result channels
Promise.race needs first-settled ordering and liveness proof
Swift continuation bridges suspend and resume through a callback-settled future-like result channel
asyncio.run drives a coroutine to completion with scheduling, result-channel, and exception boundaries
Exact-import-backed Java ScheduledExecutorService schedule calls add timer-backed task scheduling and Future result channels
Exact-import-backed Java ScheduledExecutorService repeating schedules expose interval lifecycle and cancellation boundaries
Exact-import-backed Java ExecutorService invokeAll waits for all submitted tasks and returns Future result channels
Promise.allSettled needs settled-record channel and shape proof
asyncio gather needs all-completion, result-channel, cancellation, and exception semantics
CompletableFuture settled factories create fulfilled or exceptional future channels
asyncio task APIs create scheduler, cancellation, and handle lifecycle boundaries
setjmp/longjmp is non-local control flow, not ordinary return
CompletableFuture async factories schedule executor callbacks and return future handles
Swift Task.yield yields to the task scheduler and must not collapse into sync value equivalence
scheduler.yield needs microtask/order proof
Future-like receivers need settlement continuation and callback demand/effect proof
import-backed tokio/futures/futures_util join-style macros need all-completion result-channel proof
asyncio.shield changes cancellation propagation while preserving an awaitable result channel
qualified runtime select style macros need first-completion, cancellation, and result-channel proof
CompletableFuture.allOf needs all-completion and exceptional completion proof
Exact-import-backed Java ExecutorService invokeAny exposes first-success completion, cancellation, and exception boundaries
asyncio wait needs completion-selection, result-channel, cancellation, and exception semantics
asyncio.wait_for adds timer and cancellation/liveness boundaries around an awaitable result channel
import-backed tokio/async-std spawn bindings introduce scheduler, cancellation, and join-handle boundaries
asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe schedules a coroutine onto another loop and returns a future handle
import-backed asyncio sleep bindings create timer-backed scheduling boundaries
Promise.any needs first-fulfilled and AggregateError rejection proof
asyncio.to_thread schedules a callback on a worker thread and returns an awaitable result channel
